# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.38.0` cycle (Bookmark tracking verification, [66]):

1. 0.38.0-1: wire `common::verify_tracking` into setup commands
   (`init`, `clone`, `test-fixture`). [66]
1. 0.38.0-2: wire `common::verify_tracking` into preflight
   commands (`sync`, `push`). [66]
1. 0.38.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + done marker. [66]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every
   repo-modifying command. Caused real finalize failure
   2026-04-22 (push-app succeeded, finalize couldn't push). [63]
1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]

content.text:
All green. **Important compat note for actor-x1:**

This commit's `clone` fix is real-behavior — *new* `vc-x1 clone` invocations will now produce tracked bookmarks. But if **actor-x1 was cloned previously** (without the fix), its bookmarks may be in the non-tracking state. With 0.38.0-1 alone, nothing breaks (init/clone/test-fixture only assert at fixture/setup time, not on existing repos). But once **0.38.0-2 lands** (sync/push preflight), running `vc-x1 push` or `vc-x1 sync` on actor-x1 would error with the standard remediation. **Pre-emptive fix:** run `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R <actor-x1>` (and `-R <actor-x1>/.claude`) before pushing 0.38.0-2, or just wait for the error and follow its remediation.

**Summary of 0.38.0-1:**

- **`src/clone.rs`** — Step 3/4: real fix. After `jj git init --colocate`, add `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin`, then `verify_tracking` assertion. Empirical probe confirmed colocate-after-git-clone leaves bookmarks untracked (jj's own hint says so explicitly).
- **`src/init.rs`** — Step 10: 2-line sanity assertion. Existing `jj bookmark track` calls already correct; verify is a no-op on happy path.
- **`src/test_fixture.rs`** — Step 7: 2-line sanity assertion. Existing pushes establish tracking as a side effect; verify confirms.
- **`notes/chores-06.md`** — `### 0.38.0-1` promoted from TBD stub to filled, documenting the empirical answer to chores-06 #63's open question.
- **`notes/todo.md`** — `## In Progress` shrinks from 3 items to 2 (-1 removed); Done gains the -1 bullet.

Pre-commit clean: fmt, clippy, 204 tests pass. Installed and version reports `0.38.0-1`.

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content.text:
Won't fail. Two reasons:

1. **0.38.0-2 hasn't shipped yet** — we're at 0.38.0-1, which only added tracking-verify to setup commands (init/clone/test-fixture). Push's code path is unchanged. So even if actor-x1's bookmarks were untracked, push wouldn't notice (until we land -2).
2. **Even after -2 lands, tracked state passes the check.** Your `jj bookmark list --tracked` output shows both `main` bookmarks are tracking `@origin` cleanly. `verify_tracking`'s job is to catch the *non-*tracking case. Tracked → ok → silent pass.

To actually see the new error fire, you'd need both:
- 0.38.0-2 installed (we ship that next), AND
- A bookmark in non-tracking state (manually untrack via `jj bookmark untrack main@origin -R <repo>`, or fetch into a fresh workspace — per the [63] cross-machine scenario)

If you want to dogfood the failure path, easiest is: push 0.38.0-2 first, then `jj bookmark untrack main@origin -R .` in actor-x1 (or `.claude`), then `vc-x1 push main` and watch the preflight error with the remediation command. After observing, re-track and push normally.

message.content: I'm doing vc-x1 and actor-x1 on the same machine so it's "shipping" locally :)

content.text:
Ha — fair, "ship" = `cargo install --path .` here. Same-machine just means the rollout latency is zero, doesn't change the failure-mode analysis: tracked state passes, non-tracked state errors. The cross-machine scenario in chores-06 #63 is what *creates* non-tracking state organically; on one machine, you'd manually untrack to reproduce.

So the practical path if you want to watch it fire after -2 lands:

```
jj bookmark untrack main@origin -R <some-actor-x1-repo>
vc-x1 push main   # from actor-x1 — preflight fails with remediation
jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R <same-repo>
vc-x1 push main   # works normally
```

Want me to proceed with the 0.38.0-1 push + commit message proposal so we can get to -2 and try this?

Proposed commit message:

**TITLE:**
```
fix: clone tracks bookmarks; verify in setup (0.38.0-1)
```

**BODY:**
```
Second step of the 0.38.0 multi-step rollout. Wires
common::verify_tracking into the three setup commands. One real
fix (clone) plus two sanity assertions (init, test-fixture).

Empirical finding: `jj git init --colocate` after `git clone`
does NOT auto-establish bookmark tracking — jj's own hint says
to run `jj bookmark track` explicitly. Without this fix, every
fresh vc-x1 clone left both repos in a non-tracking state that
the upcoming 0.38.0-2 push/sync preflights would reject.

- src/clone.rs — Step 3/4: after each `jj git init --colocate`,
  add `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin` then assert via
  verify_tracking. Real fix.
- src/init.rs — Step 10: verify_tracking assertion after each
  existing track call. Sanity check, no-op on healthy path.
- src/test_fixture.rs — Step 7: verify_tracking assertion after
  each `jj git push --bookmark main`. Sanity check.
- notes/chores-06.md — `### 0.38.0-1` promoted from TBD stub to
  filled, capturing the empirical finding.
- notes/todo.md — Done bullet for this step + In Progress
  shrinks to 2 items (-2 + release).
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main`?

message.content: y, but one other thought, after pushing -2 we'll dog food it here first!!

content.text: Good plan — dogfood -2 here first (untrack a bookmark, watch the error fire, re-track and push normally) before applying to actor-x1. I'll set that up after -2 ships and stop there for your direction.

763	fn run_stage(
764	    root: &Path,
765	    stage: Stage,
766	    state: &mut PushState,
767	    args: &PushArgs,
768	    layout: &StateLayout,
769	) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
770	    match stage {
771	        Stage::Preflight => stage_preflight(root, args),
772	        Stage::Review => stage_review(root, args),
773	        Stage::Message => stage_message(root, state, args, layout),
774	        Stage::CommitApp => stage_commit_app(root, state, args),
775	        Stage::CommitClaude => stage_commit_claude(root, state, args),
776	        Stage::BookmarkBoth => stage_bookmark_both(root, state, args),

779	    }
780	}
781	
782	/// Preflight: `vc-x1 sync --check && cargo fmt && cargo clippy
783	/// -D warnings && cargo test`.
784	///
785	/// Sync runs in `--check` mode so divergence with the remote is
786	/// surfaced before we burn cargo cycles, but **not** auto-resolved —
787	/// a rebase mid-push is exactly the kind of unsupervised mutation
788	/// the two approval gates exist to prevent. If sync reports action
789	/// needed, preflight errors and the user resolves explicitly with
790	/// `vc-x1 sync --no-check` before re-running push. The cargo steps
791	/// match CLAUDE.md's pre-commit checklist (minus `cargo install` /
792	/// retest, which are project-specific). All subprocesses run in the
793	/// workspace root so cargo picks up the right `Cargo.toml`. Skipped
794	/// in `--dry-run` since `cargo fmt` writes files.
795	fn stage_preflight(root: &Path, args: &PushArgs) -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
796	    if args.dry_run {
797	        info!("push preflight: [dry-run] would run vc-x1 sync --check / cargo fmt / clippy / test");
798	        return Ok(());
799	    }

input.old_string:
### 0.38.0-2: wire into preflight commands (TBD)

Wire `common::verify_tracking` into `sync` and `push` preflight:

- `sync`: detect + error in preflight before any fetch/rebase
  attempt.
- `push`: detect + error in preflight before any mutation
  (commit, bookmark move, push). The existing `vc-x1 sync
  --check` step that push runs first will already cover sync's
  side; this adds the explicit per-bookmark check that
  push-app needs before crossing the remote boundary.

`finalize` already calls `verify_tracking` (now via the shared
helper in 0.38.0-0); no change needed in -2.
input.new_string:
### 0.38.0-2: wire into preflight commands

Wired `common::verify_tracking` into the two preflight commands.
With `finalize` already covered (0.38.0-0), the full set of
repo-modifying commands is now under the same check.

**`sync` (sync.rs `sync_repos`):** added a verify loop at the top
— for each repo in the operation, verify tracking on
`args.bookmark` (defaults to `main`). Runs before snapshot
collection, before fetch, before any state mutation. If any repo
errors, sync returns `Err` and the standard cross-repo revert
path doesn't fire (nothing to revert — we hadn't snapshotted
yet).

**`push` (push.rs `stage_preflight`):** added the verify call as
the first non-dry-run step, before `vc-x1 sync --check`. Verifies
both repos (`root` and `claude_path(root)`) on `state.bookmark`.
Plumbed `state` through to `stage_preflight` (was previously
`(root, args)`; now `(root, state, args)` matching other stage
fns). Belt-and-suspenders with sync's own check — `vc-x1 sync
--check` would catch it too, but push's explicit check makes the
contract local to push and fails fast before delegating.

**`finalize`:** unchanged — already calls `verify_tracking`
(promoted to common.rs in 0.38.0-0).

Behavior for healthy repos: zero observable change (silent pass).
For non-tracking remote refs: errors before any mutation, with
the standard `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>`
remediation. All 204 tests pass — fixtures use the
`test_fixture` flow which establishes tracking via push, so the
new sync/push checks see the happy path.

- `src/sync.rs` — verify loop at top of `sync_repos`.
- `src/push.rs` — `stage_preflight` signature gains `state`,
  dispatcher arm updated, two `verify_tracking` calls added
  before `vc-x1 sync --check`.
- `notes/chores-06.md` — promote `### 0.38.0-2` from TBD to
  filled (this).
- `notes/todo.md` — Done entry + `## In Progress` shrinks to
  one item (release).

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.38.0` cycle (Bookmark tracking verification, [66]):

1. 0.38.0-2: wire `common::verify_tracking` into preflight
   commands (`sync`, `push`). [66]
1. 0.38.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + done marker. [66]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every
   repo-modifying command. Caused real finalize failure
   2026-04-22 (push-app succeeded, finalize couldn't push). [63]
1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]

- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]

content.text:
**Summary of 0.38.0-2:**

- **`src/sync.rs`** — `sync_repos` gets a verify loop at the top. Each repo verified for `args.bookmark` before snapshot/fetch.
- **`src/push.rs`** — `stage_preflight` signature gains `state` (was `(root, args)`, now `(root, state, args)`); dispatcher arm updated. Two `verify_tracking` calls added at the top of preflight (app + `.claude` on `state.bookmark`), running before `vc-x1 sync --check`. Belt-and-suspenders with sync's check; push owns its own contract.
- **`src/finalize.rs`** — unchanged (already covered in 0.38.0-0).
- **`notes/chores-06.md`** — `### 0.38.0-2` promoted from TBD to filled.
- **`notes/todo.md`** — `## In Progress` shrinks to one item (release); Done gains the -2 bullet.

Pre-commit clean: fmt, clippy, 204 tests pass. Installed; `vc-x1 --version` reports `0.38.0-2`.

**Compat note:** post-push, `vc-x1 sync` and `vc-x1 push` will both error if their target bookmark has any non-tracking remote ref. Healthy state (everything tracked) is silent pass — confirmed by your earlier `jj bookmark list --tracked` on actor-x1, both repos clean.

Per your plan, after this push lands we'll dogfood the failure path here in vc-x1 (untrack a bookmark, run push/sync, watch the error, re-track and push normally) before applying anything to actor-x1.

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content.text:
Proposed commit message:

**TITLE:**
```
feat: tracking preflight in sync + push (0.38.0-2)
```

**BODY:**
```
Third step of the 0.38.0 multi-step rollout. Wires
common::verify_tracking into sync's and push's preflight so both
detect non-tracking remote refs before any mutation. With
finalize already covered (0.38.0-0), the full set of
repo-modifying commands is now under the same check.

Healthy state (everything tracked) → silent pass. Non-tracking
remote ref → fast error in preflight before fetch / commit /
push, with the standard `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r>
-R <repo>` remediation.

- src/sync.rs — verify loop at top of `sync_repos`, before
  snapshot collection, before any fetch.
- src/push.rs — `stage_preflight` signature gains `state`;
  dispatcher arm updated. Two `verify_tracking` calls (app and
  `.claude` on `state.bookmark`) before `vc-x1 sync --check`.
  Push owns its own preflight contract; sync's check is then
  redundant-but-cheap.
- notes/chores-06.md — `### 0.38.0-2` promoted from TBD to
  filled. New `# References` block at the bottom maps inline
  `[N]` refs to GitHub URLs for clickable navigation +
  copy-paste.
- notes/todo.md — Done bullet for this step + In Progress
  shrinks to one item (release).
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main`?

After this lands, the dogfood is staged and ready: untrack a bookmark in vc-x1 (e.g. `jj bookmark untrack main@origin -R .`), run `vc-x1 push main` or `vc-x1 sync`, watch the new preflight error fire with the remediation command, then re-track and push normally. I'll wait for your signal to proceed with that.

content.text:
Dogfood ✓ — all three behaviors as designed:

1. **Untracked state → loud error with exact remediation:**
   ```
   error: bookmark 'main' has non-tracking remote 'main@origin' — 
   run `jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R .` to fix
   ```
   Exit 1. Copy-paste the suggested command verbatim and it works.

2. **bm-track also caught it** in its entry/exit lines: `app(main)=NOT_TRACKED, .claude(main)=tracked` — diagnostic and preflight signals agree.

3. **Re-track + retry → silent clean pass** (`sync: 2 repos, all bookmarks up-to-date`, exit 0).

Side observation — jj's `<bookmark>@<remote>` syntax is deprecated; jj prefers `<bookmark> --remote=<remote>`. Our generated remediation message already uses the modern form (`jj bookmark track main --remote=origin -R .`). My ad-hoc untrack/track commands used the old form and got jj's warning, but that's an unrelated nit.

Push's preflight uses the same `verify_tracking` helper, so it'd error identically — didn't probe that path since it'd require a real push cycle to exercise (commit + bookmark + remote moves), which is more invasive than warranted.

Confidence is high to apply to actor-x1 if/when needed. Next move?

content.text:
Nice — that's the cwd-independence behavior chores-06 #65 captured ("scope names are workspace-anchored, not cwd-relative"). Sync correctly walks up from `.claude` to find the workspace and operates on both repos, AND the error's path string is cwd-relative so `-R .` paste-and-run works directly from the cwd you're in. Both desired behaviors composing cleanly.

Three pieces of evidence now in hand:
- Workspace root cwd, app fail → `-R .`
- Workspace root cwd, .claude fail → `-R .claude`
- `.claude` cwd, .claude fail → `-R .` (cwd-relative)

All three remediations are directly paste-and-run from the cwd you're in. Solid validation of both #63 (tracking verification) and #65 (cwd independence).

Still open from my prior message: **0.38.0 release commit now (close out the cycle)**, or pivot? Lean unchanged — release commit. Add a brief dogfood-evidence recap to the close-out subsection so it's captured permanently.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every
   repo-modifying command. Caused real finalize failure
   2026-04-22 (push-app succeeded, finalize couldn't push). [63]
1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility

input.new_string:
### 0.38.0: release

Release commit closing out the multi-step cycle. Recap of what
landed across -0/-1/-2:

- **0.38.0-0** (refactor): promoted finalize's
  `find_non_tracking_remote` + tracking-preflight logic into
  `common::find_non_tracking_remote` + `common::verify_tracking`.
  4 parser tests moved with it. Behavior unchanged.
- **0.38.0-1** (fix + assertions): wired `verify_tracking` into
  setup commands. Real fix in `clone` (added explicit
  `jj bookmark track` after `jj git init --colocate` —
  empirically confirmed colocate doesn't auto-track). Sanity
  assertions in `init` and `test-fixture`.
- **0.38.0-2** (feat): wired `verify_tracking` into preflight
  commands (`sync`, `push`). Both detect non-tracking remote refs
  before any mutation, with the standard
  `jj bookmark track <b> --remote=<r> -R <repo>` remediation.

Final coverage: every repo-modifying command (`init`, `clone`,
`test-fixture`, `sync`, `push`, `finalize`) verifies bookmark
tracking either as a setup post-condition or as a preflight gate.

**Dogfood validation (2026-04-23/24).** Three call-site / cwd
combinations exercised on both this repo and a fresh `tf-1/work`
test fixture:

- workspace root cwd, app side untracked → error with `-R .`
- workspace root cwd, `.claude` side untracked → error with
  `-R .claude`
- `.claude` cwd, `.claude` side untracked → error with `-R .`
  (cwd-relative; paste-and-run from current cwd)

All three produce error messages whose embedded
`jj bookmark track …` command is directly copy-paste-able from
the cwd the user is in. The cwd-relative path resolution falls
out of how each command calls `verify_tracking(repo, …)` with the
repo path it's already using — no special cwd handling needed.
Validates both the tracking design ([63]) and the cwd-independence
claim ([65]).

- `notes/chores-06.md` — this `### 0.38.0` close-out subsection.
- `notes/todo.md` — `## In Progress` cleared; Done bullet for
  `0.38.0` (the cycle marker).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.38.0` cycle (Bookmark tracking verification, [66]):

1. 0.38.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + done marker. [66]

## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every
   repo-modifying command. Caused real finalize failure
   2026-04-22 (push-app succeeded, finalize couldn't push). [63]
1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. Non-tracking-remote bookmark detection across every
   repo-modifying command. Caused real finalize failure
   2026-04-22 (push-app succeeded, finalize couldn't push). [63]
1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]

- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]

content.text:
Squash done — both sides force-pushed, changeIDs preserved (`nvzoropr` app, `msuszlnl` .claude — unchanged from 0.38.0). The todo cleanup is now folded into the 0.38.0 commit.

**Proposed plan for #2 + #3: 0.39.0 multi-step (Push hardening — state + stage sanity).**

Per chores-06 [61]'s recommended implementation order:

| Step | Scope | Why this slicing |
|---|---|---|
| `0.39.0-0` | State-sanity preflight on resume | Broader guard, less invasive — verifies `state.app_chid` still exists, `main` bookmark at `state.app_chid`'s commit, `state.claude_chid` consistent with `.claude` WC. On mismatch: loud `"state is stale — run vc-x1 push --restart"` error. |
| `0.39.0-1` | Stage-prereq verification + honest completion | Per-stage "what I expect" guards (e.g. WC-dirty for commit-app, bookmark at specific commit for bookmark-both). `"Completed all stages"` only prints when stages actually ran or verified-already-done. |
| `0.39.0` | Release | Recap + dogfood evidence (induce stale state, watch state-sanity fire). |

Both fix the same incident family (silent false success after stale state) and the design says: "Both should land before the `--squash` work to avoid building on a known-fragile resume path" — so this clears the way for the eventual `--squash` work too.

Single-step alternative: all in one commit. Doable but ~200-400 LoC across multiple stages — multi-step gives clearer review and per-step dogfood checkpoints.

OK to proceed with multi-step 0.39.0, starting with `0.39.0-0` (state-sanity preflight)?
Starting 0.39.0-0 (state-sanity preflight). First exploring push.rs's resume entry point and PushState structure.

input.command: grep -n 'fn run_from\|fn cmd_run\|PushState {\|struct PushState\|pub stage:\|pub bookmark:\|pub started_at:\|pub app_chid:\|pub claude_chid:\|pub claude_had_changes' src/push.rs | head -25

content.content:
253	#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
254	pub struct PushState {
255	    /// State-file format version; must match `STATE_FORMAT_VERSION`
256	    /// to be considered readable.
257	    pub version: u32,
258	    /// The next stage to execute on resume.
259	    pub stage: Stage,
260	    /// Bookmark being advanced by this run (persisted so resume
261	    /// doesn't need `--bookmark` again).
262	    pub bookmark: String,
263	    /// ISO-8601 UTC timestamp captured when the state was first
264	    /// written. Informational — helps the user spot stale state.
265	    pub started_at: String,
266	    /// App-repo changeID captured at `message` stage (before
267	    /// `commit-app` runs). Stable across `jj commit` — becomes the
268	    /// chid of the just-committed change. Used when composing the
269	    /// `.claude` commit's ochid trailer. Added in 0.37.0-2.
270	    pub app_chid: Option<String>,
271	    /// `.claude` repo changeID used by the app-repo commit's ochid
272	    /// trailer. Either the pre-commit `@` chid (when `.claude` has
273	    /// pending changes — becomes `@-` after commit) or the current
274	    /// `@-` chid (when `.claude` is clean — stays stable). Added in
275	    /// 0.37.0-2.
276	    pub claude_chid: Option<String>,
277	    /// Whether `.claude`'s working copy had changes at `message`
278	    /// time. Decides whether `commit-claude` actually runs or
279	    /// skips. Added in 0.37.0-2.
280	    pub claude_had_changes: Option<bool>,

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.39.0` cycle (Push hardening: state + stage sanity, [67]):

1. 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. [67]
1. 0.39.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + dogfood validation. [67]


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]

## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (0.39.0)

Implementation cycle for [61] (Push hardening: state + stage
sanity design). Multi-step rollout addressing the silent
false-success failure mode that surfaced in the 0.37.1 dogfood
incident: push parked at finalize-claude after a failure;
out-of-band recovery moved the world forward; subsequent push
resumed at the parked stage, no-op'd, and falsely declared
"completed all stages" while WC still held uncommitted changes.

- **0.39.0-0** — state-sanity preflight on resume (broader
  guard, less invasive).
- **0.39.0-1** — stage-prereq verification + honest completion
  (per-stage "what I expect" guards).
- **0.39.0** — release commit + dogfood evidence.

### 0.39.0-0: state-sanity preflight on resume

Added `verify_state_sanity(root, state)` in push.rs. Runs at the
top of `run_from`, before the initial state save and before any
stage executes. Three checks per [61] design:

1. `state.app_chid` resolves in the app repo (errors if
   abandoned or rewritten).
2. After `bookmark-both` has run (`state.stage ∈ {PushApp,
   FinalizeClaude}`), the bookmark points at a commit whose chid
   matches `state.app_chid` (catches "world moved forward via
   manual recovery" — the 0.37.1 incident).
3. `state.claude_chid` resolves in `.claude` (when set).

A fresh state (no chids yet) is a no-op — nothing to verify.
The check on (2) is gated on stage because between `message`
and `bookmark-both`, `app_chid` is set but the bookmark hasn't
been moved yet — naively comparing would always fail.

On mismatch: error with the exact `vc-x1 push <bookmark>
--restart` remediation. Example shape:

> `state-sanity: app_chid 'xxx' has been abandoned or no longer
> resolves in the app repo. State is stale — run \`vc-x1 push
> main --restart\` to clear.`

All 204 tests pass — fixtures use happy paths (fresh state +
clean fixture) so the new check is a no-op there. Failure paths
get manual dogfood at the 0.39.0 release commit.

Test-strategy note: explicit unit tests for the comparison
logic weren't added because most of the behavior is jj-shelling
(hard to mock without infrastructure). Existing test suite
covers the happy path; failure paths get manual dogfood.

- `src/push.rs` — new `verify_state_sanity` fn; called from
  `run_from` before initial state save.
- `notes/chores-06.md` — new `## Push hardening: state + stage
  sanity (0.39.0)` parent + this `### 0.39.0-0` filled
  sub-section + `### 0.39.0-1` and `### 0.39.0` TBD stubs.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done bullet for this step + `## In Progress`
  populated with remaining 0.39.0 steps + new `[67]` reference.

### 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification (TBD)

Per-stage prerequisites: each stage declares what it expects
(e.g. WC dirty for `commit-app`; bookmark at specific commit
for `bookmark-both`; etc.); the dispatcher checks before
running. "Completed all stages" prints only when stages
genuinely ran or were verified-already-done — not when skipped
without verification (the original 0.37.1 false-success
symptom).

Likely shape: a `stage_prereq(stage, root, state) -> Result<…>`
helper, called by `run_from` before each `run_stage` call.
Track per-stage outcome (ran / verified-already-done / skipped)
so the final completion message is accurate.

### 0.39.0: release (TBD)

Release commit closing out the cycle: recap of -0/-1 + dogfood
evidence (induce stale state → state-sanity fires; induce
stage-prereq violation → stage-prereq fires).

### 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification (TBD)

(e.g. WC dirty for `commit-app`; bookmark at specific commit
for `bookmark-both`; etc.); the dispatcher checks before
running. "Completed all stages" prints only when stages
genuinely ran or were verified-already-done — not when skipped
without verification (the original 0.37.1 false-success
symptom).

Likely shape: a `stage_prereq(stage, root, state) -> Result<…>`
helper, called by `run_from` before each `run_stage` call.
Track per-stage outcome (ran / verified-already-done / skipped)
so the final completion message is accurate.

### 0.39.0: release (TBD)

Release commit closing out the cycle: recap of -0/-1 + dogfood
evidence (induce stale state → state-sanity fires; induce
stage-prereq violation → stage-prereq fires).

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]

- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]

content.text:
All green. **Summary of 0.39.0-0:**

- **`src/push.rs`** — new `verify_state_sanity(root, state)` fn (~95 lines incl. doc comment). Three checks per [61] design:
  1. `state.app_chid` resolves in app repo.
  2. After `bookmark-both` (state.stage ∈ PushApp/FinalizeClaude), bookmark's chid matches `state.app_chid`.
  3. `state.claude_chid` resolves in `.claude` (when set).
  
  Called at top of `run_from` before initial state save. Fresh state (no chids) → no-op.

- **`notes/chores-06.md`** — new `## Push hardening: state + stage sanity (0.39.0)` parent + filled `### 0.39.0-0` + TBD stubs for `-1` and release. Added `[61]` and `[67]` to the references block.

- **`notes/todo.md`** — `## In Progress` populated with `-1` and release entries. Done bullet added.

**Compat for actor-x1:** new check runs at top of `run_from` in push. For typical flow (no saved state file or fresh state) → no-op. Only impact: if actor-x1 had a stale push state file from an interrupted push, the new check would flag it with the standard `--restart` remediation. Since you've been completing pushes cleanly there, no fallout expected.

Test note: explicit unit tests not added — comparison logic is jj-shelling-heavy and hard to mock. 204 existing tests pass (happy paths use fresh state). Failure paths get manual dogfood at the 0.39.0 release.

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content: Goal: roll out push correctness hardening as 0.39.0. Current task: 0.39.0-0 done — added verify_state_sanity preflight to push. Next action: review the staged changes and approve the commit so I can push.

content.text:
Good catch — `#anchor-name` works in any markdown viewer (local editors, GitHub render) and is cleaner. Full URLs only added value for "copy-paste into browser" but they break on raw-markdown views and add noise.

Updating the references block — anchor-only for self-refs (chores-06 → chores-06), and `/notes/chores-05.md#...` for the one cross-file ref ([57]) to match todo.md's workspace-root-absolute pattern.

content.text:
Right — the root cause. `[61]` only resolves if the reader has the references table memorized.

Source-code design refs need three things to be useful: **descriptive name** (what), **path** (where), **URL** (clickable). Same pattern we just landed in `src/sync.rs:142`:

```rust
/// Three checks per the "Push hardening: state + stage sanity" design:
///   https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/chores-06.md#push-hardening-state--stage-sanity-design
```

Self-contained and clickable — reader doesn't need to know what `[61]` means.

Inventory of similar refs in source:

| File | Line | Current | Quality |
|---|---|---|---|
| sync.rs | 142 | section name + URL | ✓ good (just landed in 0.38.0-2) |
| push.rs | 4 | `notes/chores-05.md > Add push subcommand (0.37.0)` | name + path, no URL |
| push.rs | 121 | `notes/chores-05.md` | path only, no name |
| push.rs | 645 | `chores-06 [61] design` | opaque ref ✗ |
| push.rs | 1219 | `chores-06 [61] design` | opaque ref ✗ |

Two paths:

1. **Fix only the two new `[61]` refs in this 0.39.0-0 commit** (the immediate concern). Lines 4 and 121 stay as-is — they're not as bad and they're outside this commit's scope. Old patterns can drift to the new convention organically.
2. **Fix all four push.rs refs in this commit** for consistency, plus add the convention to CLAUDE.md as a short note (separate small commit later).

Lean: **option 1** for this commit (focused), then **codify in CLAUDE.md next** so future code uses the pattern automatically. Open to option 2 if you'd rather sweep now.

Which?

message.content: I'm thinking fixing all of them in a separate commit, but in source code it should point to "latest" or ??

content.text:
Yes, `blob/main/...` (latest) is right for source-code design refs. Reasoning:

- Source code is the **implementation that tracks the spec**. If the design evolves, the link should show the new spec — that's what the code should be aligning to.
- Self-maintaining — no per-commit URL maintenance.
- Pinning to a commit hash is right for *historical* refs ("this was the design when the incident happened"), but for ongoing implementation refs you want "see current design".

**Tradeoff to flag:** stable section names matter. If a design subsection gets renamed, the `#anchor` breaks (silent — you don't notice until someone clicks). Convention worth codifying: don't rename design subsection headers once landed; if the topic evolves substantially, add a new subsection rather than rewriting the old one's name.

Three things to capture in CLAUDE.md when we codify (small follow-up commit):

1. Source-code design refs use the form: section name + `blob/main/.../chores-NN.md#anchor` URL.
2. Markdown-internal refs use anchor-only (`#anchor`) for self-file or `/notes/...md#anchor` for cross-file.
3. Don't rename design subsection headers post-landing (link stability).

For this 0.39.0-0 commit: leave the two `[61]` refs as-is (they'll get fixed in the dedicated cleanup commit per your plan). Move forward with commit message proposal?

# References

[57]: /notes/chores-05.md#capture-squash-mode--scope-design-for-push-0374
input.new_string:
## Source-code design ref convention (design)

When source code references a design captured in `notes/`, the
ref should be useful on its own — three things matter: the
descriptive **section name** (what the design is), the **URL**
to it (where to find it), and **clickable form** (so the reader
navigates without a decoder ring). The opaque `[N]` syntax used
in markdown notes doesn't translate to source — `(per chores-06
[61] design)` is unhelpful without the references table
memorized.

**Pattern (decided):**

```rust
/// One-line gist per the "Section Name" design:
///   https://github.com/winksaville/vc-x1/blob/main/notes/<file>.md#anchor
```

Full URL (`blob/main/...`) is right for source code because:

- Source has no markdown rendering — anchor-only `#name` doesn't
  navigate from a `.rs` file.
- It's clickable in IDEs that recognize URLs.
- Tracking `main` (not a commit hash) means the link auto-shows
  the *current* design, which is what implementation code should
  be aligning to. Pinning to a commit hash is right for
  historical refs ("the design at the time of this incident")
  but not for ongoing implementation refs.

**Stable section names matter.** Once a design subsection lands,
don't rename its `## …` header — links break silently (the URL
still resolves to the file but the anchor goes nowhere). If a
topic evolves substantially, add a new subsection rather than
rewriting the old one's name.

**Sweep targets** (when this design lands as code):

- `src/sync.rs:142` — already correct (landed 0.38.0-2).
- `src/push.rs:4` — name + path, no URL. Upgrade.
- `src/push.rs:121` — path only, no name. Upgrade.
- `src/push.rs:645` — opaque `[61] design`. Upgrade.
- `src/push.rs:1219` — opaque `[61] design`. Upgrade.

**CLAUDE.md codification** (same commit as the source sweep):

1. Source-code design refs use section name + `blob/main/...` URL.
2. Markdown-internal refs use anchor-only (`#anchor`) for
   self-file or `/notes/<file>.md#anchor` for cross-file.
3. Don't rename design subsection headers post-landing
   (link stability).

# References

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.39.0` cycle (Push hardening: state + stage sanity, [67]):

1. 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. [67]
1. 0.39.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + dogfood validation. [67]


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.39.0` cycle (Push hardening: state + stage sanity, [67]):

1. 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. [67]
1. 0.39.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + dogfood validation. [67]


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

feat: push state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0)

```
First step of the 0.39.0 multi-step cycle (Push hardening:
state + stage sanity). Adds verify_state_sanity at the top of
run_from, before any stage executes. Catches the silent
false-success failure mode that surfaced in the 0.37.1 dogfood
incident.

- state.app_chid still resolves in the app repo.
- After bookmark-both has run (state.stage ∈ PushApp |
  FinalizeClaude), the bookmark's chid matches state.app_chid.
- state.claude_chid still resolves in .claude (when set).

Fresh state (no chids) → no-op. Mismatch → loud error with the
exact `vc-x1 push <bookmark> --restart` remediation. Failure
paths get manual dogfood at the 0.39.0 release commit.

source-code design ref convention — surfaced during review when
the verify_state_sanity doc comment used the opaque `[N]` ref
syntax that doesn't translate from markdown to source. Sweep
deferred to a follow-up commit.

- notes/todo.md — Done bullet for -0; In Progress populated
  with remaining 0.39.0 steps; new Todo entry for the
  source-code design ref sweep + new [67] and [68] refs.
```

/// Return the 12-character change ID for `rev` in `repo`.
fn get_change_id(repo: &Path, rev: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {

lines: +/// Verify the world matches the saved state after all stages have run.
+/// runs after the stage loop completes and confirms the post-conditions
+/// are real — directly addresses the 0.37.1 false-success symptom
+/// (push declared "completed all stages" while WC still held
+/// uncommitted changes from a stale-state resume).
+/// 1. App's bookmark is at a commit whose chid matches `state.app_chid`.
+/// 2. App's working copy has no uncommitted changes (commit-app should
+///    have captured anything that was there).
+///    `.claude`'s WC may legitimately have new session writes from the
+///    push run itself — finalize-claude (detached) handles those — so
+/// On any mismatch: returns `Err`. The caller surfaces as a loud
+/// warning rather than blocking — push has already crossed the remote
+/// boundary, work is live on origin, the warning prompts the user to
