### 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification (TBD)

Per-stage prerequisites: each stage declares what it expects
(e.g. WC dirty for `commit-app`; bookmark at specific commit
for `bookmark-both`; etc.); the dispatcher checks before
running. "Completed all stages" prints only when stages
genuinely ran or were verified-already-done — not when skipped
without verification (the original 0.37.1 false-success
symptom).

Likely shape: a `stage_prereq(stage, root, state) -> Result<…>`
helper, called by `run_from` before each `run_stage` call.
Track per-stage outcome (ran / verified-already-done / skipped)
so the final completion message is accurate.

### 0.39.0: release (TBD)
input.new_string:
### 0.39.0-1: honest completion via post-completion verification

Added `verify_completion_sanity(root, state)` in push.rs — the
post-loop counterpart to `verify_state_sanity` from -0. Runs
after all stages complete; verifies the world matches the saved
state before declaring "completed all stages".

Three checks:

1. App bookmark's chid matches `state.app_chid`.
2. App WC has no uncommitted changes (commit-app should have
   captured anything that was there). **This is the direct
   counter to the 0.37.1 false-success symptom** — push declared
   "completed all stages" while WC still held uncommitted
   changes from a stale-state resume.
3. `.claude` bookmark's chid matches `state.claude_chid` (when
   set). No `.claude` WC-clean check — `.claude` may legitimately
   have new session writes from the push run itself, which
   finalize-claude (detached) handles.

On failure: warning-only (not Err) because push has already
crossed the remote boundary by completion; rollback isn't sound.
State is cleared either way, but the success message changes:

- Pass → `push: completed all stages (verified, state cleared)`.
- Fail → `push: completed stages but post-completion verification
  failed: <reason>; state cleared anyway (work landed on remote);
  investigate the discrepancy before next push`.

**Scope note — per-stage prereqs deferred to 0.39.0-2.** The
[61] design called for *both* per-stage prereq checks *and*
honest completion. Per-stage prereqs would require changing
every stage's signature (`Result<()>` → `Result<StageOutcome>`)
and threading outcomes through the dispatcher — substantially
more invasive than the post-completion check, and with
state-sanity preflight (-0) already covering the resume case,
the marginal value is lower. Captured as the new `### 0.39.0-2`
TBD subsection below.

All 204 tests pass — fixtures use happy paths (chids match, WC
clean) so the check passes silently. Failure paths get manual
dogfood at the 0.39.0 release commit.

- `src/push.rs` — new `verify_completion_sanity` fn; called from
  `run_from` after the stage loop. Success message qualified
  with "(verified)"; failure surfaces as a warning.
- `notes/chores-06.md` — promote `### 0.39.0-1` from TBD to
  filled (this) + new `### 0.39.0-2` TBD stub for the deferred
  per-stage prereq work.
- `notes/todo.md` — Done bullet for this step + `## In Progress`
  updated (added `0.39.0-2` entry, kept release).

### 0.39.0-2: per-stage prereq verification (TBD)

Deferred from `### 0.39.0-1` for scope. Per-stage prerequisites
— each stage declares what it expects (e.g. WC dirty for
`commit-app`, bookmark at specific commit for `bookmark-both`,
etc.); the dispatcher checks before running. Tracks outcome
per stage (ran / already-done) so the completion message can
report exactly what happened.

Likely shape: `StageOutcome` enum (`Ran`, `AlreadyDone`); each
stage_xxx returns `Result<StageOutcome>`; dispatcher accumulates;
completion message lists outcomes per stage.

May be skipped if dogfood of -1's post-completion check shows
sufficient coverage — the per-stage prereqs are belt-and-
suspenders, the post-completion is the meaningful end-to-end
guard. Decide before the 0.39.0 release commit.

### 0.39.0: release (TBD)

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.39.0` cycle (Push hardening: state + stage sanity, [67]):

1. 0.39.0-1: stage-prereq verification + honest completion. [67]
1. 0.39.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + dogfood validation. [67]


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]

content.text:
**Summary of 0.39.0-1:**

- **`src/push.rs`** — new `verify_completion_sanity` fn (parallel to `verify_state_sanity` from -0). Three checks: app bookmark chid matches `state.app_chid`; app WC clean; `.claude` bookmark chid matches `state.claude_chid`. Called after the stage loop completes. Success → `push: completed all stages (verified, state cleared)`. Failure → warning (not error, since work is already on remote) + state cleared anyway + "investigate the discrepancy before next push".

- **`notes/chores-06.md`** — `### 0.39.0-1` filled (honest completion via post-completion verification, with scope note explaining per-stage prereq deferral). New `### 0.39.0-2 (TBD)` stub for the deferred per-stage prereq work.

- **`notes/todo.md`** — `## In Progress` updated (-1 removed, new -2 deferred entry added, release stays). Done bullet for -1.

**Scope deviation from original plan:** -1 covers the honest-completion half of the [61] design; per-stage prereq checks are deferred to a possible -2 (or may be skipped if dogfood shows -0 + -1 is sufficient).

**Compat for actor-x1:** new check runs at end of push. For healthy flow, silent pass with a slightly enriched success message (`(verified, ...)`). Only impact: if a push completes with a state mismatch, the warning fires. Low risk — 0.39.0-0 catches most resume-time issues, completion mismatches are rare in normal flows.

Pre-commit clean (fmt, clippy, 204 tests pass). Installed at 0.39.0-1.

Please review. On approval I'll prep the commit title and body for a second approval before pushing.

content: We're mid-cycle on 0.39.0 push hardening; 0.39.0-1 (honest completion via post-completion verification) is built and ready to commit. Next: approve the proposed commit title/body so I can run vc-x1 push main.

content.text:
Proposed commit message:

**TITLE:**
```
feat: push post-completion sanity check (0.39.0-1)
```

**BODY:**
```
Second step of the 0.39.0 multi-step cycle. Adds
verify_completion_sanity in push.rs — runs after the stage loop
completes, verifies the world matches the saved state before
declaring "completed all stages". Counterpart to
verify_state_sanity from -0 (which guards on resume).

Three checks:
- App bookmark's chid matches state.app_chid.
- App WC has no uncommitted changes (commit-app should have
  captured them) — direct counter to the 0.37.1 false-success
  symptom.
- .claude bookmark's chid matches state.claude_chid (when set).

On pass: success message becomes
  push: completed all stages (verified, state cleared)

On mismatch: warning (not error) — work has already crossed the
remote boundary, rollback isn't sound. State is cleared either
way; user is prompted to investigate before next push.

Scope deviation from the original [61] design plan: this commit
covers the "honest completion" half but defers per-stage prereq
checks to a new 0.39.0-2 step. Per-stage prereqs would require
changing every stage's signature and threading outcomes through
the dispatcher — substantially more invasive, and with -0
already covering the resume-state case, the marginal value is
lower. May be skipped if dogfood shows -0 + -1 is sufficient.

- src/push.rs — new verify_completion_sanity fn; called from
  run_from after the stage loop. Success message qualified with
  "(verified)"; failure surfaces as a warning.
- notes/chores-06.md — ### 0.39.0-1 promoted from TBD to
  filled; new ### 0.39.0-2 TBD stub for the deferred per-stage
  prereq work.
- notes/todo.md — Done bullet for -1; In Progress updated
  (added -2 deferred entry, kept release).
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main`?

content.text:
Now to your `validate-repo` question:

**Strong yes, makes sense as a new top-level subcommand.** It naturally consolidates everything we've been building plus other diagnostic checks. Rough shape:

```
vc-x1 validate-repo [--scope=app|other|both]
  → bookmark tracking         (uses common::verify_tracking)
  → push state freshness      (if state file exists, check vs reality)
  → ochid trailer integrity   (cross-trailer chids resolve)
  → no jj conflicts           (jj log -r conflicts())
  → workspace config sanity   (.vc-config.toml present + parseable)
  → bookmark/remote tracking matrix (richer view of [52])
  → repo working-copy state   (clean/dirty summary)

Reports: pass-or-fail per check + summary line. Exit code = number of failed checks (0 = clean).
```

**Implementation hook:** the verify_* fns currently private to push.rs (`verify_state_sanity`, `verify_completion_sanity`) would benefit from being promoted to `common.rs` — which `validate-repo` would then call. That's natural refactoring.

**Relationship to existing items:**
- `vc-x1 status` (already on the todo, item near top): operational — "what is the current state" (`jj st` across both repos).
- `vc-x1 validate-repo` (this proposal): correctness — "does the current state make sense / pass health checks".

Two distinct commands with distinct purposes. Both worth having.

**Capture path — three options:**
1. **Capture in this 0.39.0-1 commit** — small chores design subsection + todo entry. Fold in alongside the test additions.
2. **Capture in the 0.39.0 release commit** — cycle close-out gathers any new ideas the cycle surfaced.
3. **Capture in a separate small commit later** — pure design capture.

Lean: **option 1** (this commit) since you raised it during review of -1 and the verify_* promotion idea ties back to the work we just did. OK to fold in?
Adding the design + todo for validate-repo.

## `vc-x1 validate-repo` command (design)

Surfaced 2026-04-24 during the 0.39.0-1 review. A new top-level
subcommand that consolidates all the `verify_*` checks we've
built across the codebase (and adds a few more) into one
diagnostic command. Use cases: pre-flight before starting work;
CI hook; "feels off, what's wrong?" debugging.

**Proposed shape:** `vc-x1 validate-repo [--scope=app|other|both]`

**Checks** (composed from existing + new):

- Bookmark tracking — `common::verify_tracking` (0.38.0).
- Push state freshness — if `.vc-x1/push-state.toml` exists,
  run state-sanity logic (currently push-private; promote to
  `common`).
- Ochid trailer integrity — both repos' commit-body ochid
  trailers reference real changeIDs in the counterpart repo.
  No equivalent today; new code in this command.
- No jj conflicts — `jj log -r conflicts() --no-graph` returns
  empty per repo.
- Workspace config sanity — `.vc-config.toml` present,
  parseable, `path` field matches workspace structure.
- Bookmark/remote tracking matrix — richer view of [52].
- Working-copy state — clean / dirty summary per repo (sibling
  to [54]).

**Output shape:**

- One line per check: `✓ <check>` (pass) or `✗ <check>: <reason>`
  (fail).
- Summary line: `validate-repo: N/M checks passed`.
- Exit code: number of failed checks (0 = clean).

**Implementation hooks:**

- `verify_state_sanity` and `verify_completion_sanity` in
  push.rs should be promoted to `common.rs` so `validate-repo`
  can call them. Natural refactoring during implementation.
- `verify_tracking` already in `common.rs` (0.38.0).

**Relationship to other todos:**

- `vc-x1 status` (existing todo): *operational* — "what is the
  current state" (`jj st` across repos).
- `vc-x1 validate-repo` (this design): *correctness* — "does
  the current state make sense / pass health checks".
- Distinct commands, distinct concerns. Both worth having.

# References

# Todo

This file contains near term tasks with a short description
and reference links to more details.

## In Progress

Multi-step `0.39.0` cycle (Push hardening: state + stage sanity, [67]):

1. 0.39.0-2: per-stage prereq verification (deferred from -1
   for scope; may be skipped if -1's post-completion check
   shows sufficient coverage). [67]
1. 0.39.0 release: notes/doc cleanup + dogfood validation. [67]


## Todo

A markdown list of tasks to do in the near future, ordered
highest-priority first. Keep entries brief — 1-3 lines.
Detailed motivation, safety requirements, and ordering belong
in `notes/chores-NN.md` design subsections; link via `[N]` ref.

Items use lazy numbering — every entry begins with `1. `; the
markdown renderer auto-numbers them, so reorder/insert without
renumbering. Reference by displayed number ("let's work on #3").

1. vc-x1 push: state-sanity preflight on resume. Refuse with a
   loud "state is stale" error when saved state doesn't match
   reality. Surfaced by 0.37.1 dogfood. [61]
1. vc-x1 push: stage-prereq verification + honest completion.
   "Completed all stages" must reflect what actually ran. [61]
1. vc-x1 commands: support single and dual repo. Generalize via
   `--scope=app|other|both` across all commands; foundational
   for new commands and for retrofitting sync / push / finalize
   under one vocabulary. [60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--scope=app|claude|both` flag. Applies the
   generalized convention; warn on scope/WC mismatch. [57],[60]
1. vc-x1 push: `--squash` flag. Squashes WC into `@-` via
   `--ignore-immutable` and force-pushes; needs
   `--force-with-lease`-equivalent + state-sanity preflight in
   place first. [57]
1. vc-x1 push: `--message-file PATH` flag. Git-style commit
   message file (first line = title, blank, rest = body).
   Alternative to `--title` + `--body`. [58]
1. Mirror `--check` / `--no-check` onto `vc-x1 push` (forwards
   through to the preflight `vc-x1 sync` invocation).
   0.37.1 hard-codes `--check`; default stays `--check`.
1. Add `status` (alias `st`) subcommand: `jj st` across both
   repos in one shot. Uses `--scope` from day one. Natural
   home for the working-copy signal called out in [54].
1. sync: surface working-copy state in the up-to-date summary
   (per-repo pending-files count or compact stat). Wording-only
   fix shipped in 0.37.1; this is the design+impl. [54]
1. bm-track silent-when-clean refinement. Print on entry/exit
   only when state isn't fully tracked or when exit state
   differs from entry. [62]
1. "Oh shit" revert — post-success undo via `.vc-x1-ops/`
   anchor dir. Idea-stage; every repo-mutating command drops a
   pre-op snapshot, `vc-x1 undo` restores both repos. [57]
1. vc-x1 test-fixture should refuse `--path` values that resolve
   inside the current workspace root (error or warn). Dogfood
   surfaced this: `--path ./tf-1` inside the repo let jj
   snapshot the fixture's bare-git remotes — 56-file noise blob.
1. Restructure templates: replace separate `vc-template-x1` +
   `vc-template-x1.claude` repos with a single `vc-template-x1`
   that has `.claude/` as a subdir (covers `LICENSE-*` etc. for
   both sides in one place). Updates to `vc-x1 init` / `clone`
   needed for the new layout.
1. Source-code design ref sweep + CLAUDE.md codification:
   adopt section-name + `blob/main/...` URL pattern for source
   code refs to designs; codify in CLAUDE.md alongside the
   existing markdown ref conventions. Sweep targets:
   src/push.rs lines 4, 121, 645, 1219. [68]
1. Richer bookmark enumeration: per-bookmark remote presence + tracking status [52]
1. Per-line/per-thread runtime log points (future, maybe) [36]
1. Add Windows symlink support via `std::os::windows::fs::symlink_dir` [37]
1. Add "::" revision syntax for jj compatibility
1. Add -p, --parents, -c, --children so parent and child counts can be asymmetric
1. Add integration tests in tests/ for subcommands using temp jj repos (tempfile crate)
1. Fix .claude repo history: dev0 through dev2 sessions squashed into wrong commit [4],[5]
1. Add `vc-x1 setup` subcommand: completions install, .claude repo init, symlink setup [27]
1. Add dynamic revision completion via `ArgValueCompleter` (jj doesn't complete revsets either) [28],[29]

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

- CLAUDE.md refresh + memory migration (0.36.1) [49]
- Lift sync's inline test harness into shared `test_helpers` module (0.36.2) [51]
- Sync improvements: -R flag + quieter dry-run + sync-before-work discipline (0.36.3) [50]
- push subcommand scaffolding: flag surface, Stage enum, stub (0.37.0-0) [48]
- push state machine: state file, --status/--restart/--from, stage stubs (0.37.0-1) [48]
- push real stage bodies + jj-op snapshot rollback (0.37.0-2) [48]
- push integration tests + workspace-root refactor (0.37.0-3) [48]
- push interactivity: review prompt, $EDITOR, message persistence (0.37.0-4) [48]
- push polish: --dry-run, --step, non-tty detection, gitignore warning (0.37.0-5) [48]
- push docs + workflow migration — CLAUDE.md rewrite + README section (0.37.0) [48]
- First-dogfood polish for push: editor template, gitignore-fatal, sync --check, log prefix, quieter subprocess (0.37.1) [53]
- Temporary bookmark-tracking diagnostic probe on command entry/exit (0.37.2) [55]
- Fix bm-track bugs + rename + promote to permanent (0.37.3) [56]
- Capture squash-mode + scope design for push (0.37.4) [57]
- Capture --message-file design for push (0.37.5) [58]
- CLAUDE.md polish: markdown-anchor rule, shell-path brevity, state-file clearing, late-changes recipe trimmed (0.37.6) [59]
- Notes restructure: chores-06 + trim long todo entries (0.37.7) [64]
- Scope design refinements (0.37.8) [65]
- Bookmark tracking verification: shared helper + tests (0.38.0-0) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into setup commands (0.38.0-1) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: wire into preflight commands (0.38.0-2) [66]
- Bookmark tracking verification: cycle close-out + dogfood validation (0.38.0) [66]
- Push hardening: state-sanity preflight on resume (0.39.0-0) [67]
- Push hardening: honest completion via post-completion verification (0.39.0-1) [67]

# References

message.content: Any of the prereq should be done?

content.text:
Two ways to read your question — let me address both:

**"Are any prereq checks valuable enough to do now (in -1)?"** — my view: no. The post-completion check (-1) catches the actual 0.37.1 symptom (false success with dirty WC). Per-stage prereqs are belt-and-suspenders — they'd let us say "stage X already-done" in the completion summary, but don't fix new failure modes. Substantial refactor (every `stage_xxx` returns `StageOutcome`), low marginal value.

**"Should any prereqs be done at all (ever)?"** — also lean no, with a wrinkle. Here's the per-stage breakdown:

| Stage | Useful prereq? | Note |
|---|---|---|
| Preflight | n/a | always runs |
| Review | n/a | always runs |
| Message | n/a | always runs (composes msg) |
| CommitApp | maybe | jj already errors on empty WC; redundant |
| CommitClaude | maybe | already gated on `claude_had_changes`; redundant |
| BookmarkBoth | weak | jj prints "Nothing changed" for no-op move |
| PushApp | weak | jj prints "Nothing changed" for no-op push |
| FinalizeClaude | **none useful** | detached — can't know its outcome from push's perspective |

For the stages where a no-op might happen (CommitApp/Claude, BookmarkBoth, PushApp), jj's own "Nothing changed" output is already visible. The only thing per-stage prereqs add is *structured* tracking (Ran vs AlreadyDone in the completion message) — useful for audits but not for correctness.

**Recommendation:** keep the `### 0.39.0-2` TBD stub for now, but with strong lean toward *not implementing* it — flip to "skipped, not needed" at the 0.39.0 release if dogfood of -0 + -1 reveals no gap. State-sanity (-0) + post-completion (-1) is the meaningful safety pair; per-stage prereq is audit polish.

OK to proceed with this view? If yes, push -1 as-is and the release commit will document the skip.

Updated commit message reflecting the test additions + validate-repo capture:

**TITLE:**
```
feat: push post-completion sanity check (0.39.0-1)
```

**BODY:**
```
Second step of the 0.39.0 multi-step cycle. Adds
verify_completion_sanity in push.rs — runs after the stage loop
completes, verifies the world matches the saved state before
declaring "completed all stages". Counterpart to
verify_state_sanity from -0 (which guards on resume).

Three checks:
- App bookmark's chid matches state.app_chid.
- App WC is empty (uses jj_log_empty — jj diff --stat would
  false-positive with its "0 files changed" boilerplate).
  Direct counter to the 0.37.1 false-success symptom.
- .claude bookmark's chid matches state.claude_chid (when set).

On pass: success message becomes
  push: completed all stages (verified, state cleared)

On mismatch: warning (not error) — work has already crossed the
remote boundary, rollback isn't sound. State cleared either way;
user prompted to investigate before next push.

4 new integration tests cover happy path + each failure mode
(204 → 208). The dirty-WC test caught the diff-output-emptiness
bug noted above.

Scope deviations from the [61] design plan:
- Per-stage prereq checks deferred to 0.39.0-2, with strong
  lean toward "skipped, not needed" — post-completion +
  state-sanity is the meaningful safety pair; per-stage
  prereqs would be audit polish only.
- Captured ## vc-x1 validate-repo command (design) [69] —
  surfaced during -1 review as a natural extension that
  consolidates all verify_* checks into one diagnostic.

- src/push.rs — new verify_completion_sanity fn; called from
  run_from after the stage loop. 4 new integration tests in
  the integration_tests module.
- notes/chores-06.md — ### 0.39.0-1 promoted from TBD to
  filled; new ### 0.39.0-2 TBD stub; new
  ## vc-x1 validate-repo command (design) subsection [69].
- notes/todo.md — Done bullet for -1; In Progress updated;
  new Todo entry for validate-repo + new [69] reference.
```

OK to run `vc-x1 push main`?
